data.title: Pre-commit test-gate hook times out on large merges despite 'failing open' message — exit 124 blocks commit
data.description:
## What happened

The pre-commit-test-gate hook in the husky pre-commit chain times out after 10 seconds when processing large merge commits (31+ staged source files). The hook logs 'WARNING: timed out — failing open (commit allowed)' but exits with code 124, which husky interprets as failure — blocking the commit.

## Reproduction

1. Work on a feature branch with changes across 19+ source files
2. Merge origin/main which adds more changed files (31 total staged)
3. Attempt git commit — test-gate runs record-test-status.sh per-file, exceeds 10s, exits 124

## Expected behavior

Either the 10-second timeout should be increased for merge commits, or exit code 124 should be mapped to exit 0 when the 'failing open' policy applies.

## Observed behavior

Hook prints 'failing open (commit allowed)' then exits 124. Husky treats this as failure, blocking the commit.

## Workaround

Run git commit interactively (the timeout still triggers but the user can retry or the hook can be bypassed).

## Affected component

pre-commit-test-gate hook (DSO plugin)

## Security constraint

Any fix MUST NOT allow agents to bypass the test gate for normal (non-merge) commits. The test gate exists to prevent untested code from being committed. The fix should only apply to the timeout/exit-code behavior for large merge commits where tests have already been verified to pass.
